_meta.note: Segment revenue estimates are based on proportional allocation from SPGI 10-K 2024 segment disclosures applied to FY2025 total revenue of $15.336B. Exact FY2025 segment breakdown not in structured yfinance data (see _databundle.json gaps). Use as directional estimates; precise split pending 10-K 2025 filing.

total_revenue_path.driver: Subscription base (inflation-escalated) + issuance cycle recovery (Ratings transaction) + AUM-linked growth (Indices) + new AI-adjacent products (Kensho/Spark)

segments.name: S&P Global Market Intelligence
growth_drivers: Multi-year subscription contracts with 3-5% annual price escalators; renewal rates historically 90%+
Enterprise solution expansion (Capital IQ Pro, Lending Solutions, Clearance Risk)
Cross-sell of Ratings data into Market Intelligence workflow tools
Kensho AI/ML products driving new use cases in credit risk and equity research
growth_risks: Bloomberg Terminal AI expansion (Bloomberg Copilot, Bloomberg Intelligence AI) directly competing for the same workflow budget
FactSet, Refinitiv/LSEG DataStream competing at lower price points for mid-market customers
AI-native startups (AlphaSense, Tegus) growing rapidly in research workflow automation
Enterprise budget pressure may drive customers to rationalize multi-vendor data spend
segments.crux_exposure: HIGH — this is the segment where the AI disruption narrative is most directly applicable

segments.name: S&P Global Ratings
growth_drivers: Subscription revenues (~60% of Ratings) growing 6-7% per year on database access and analytics products
Transaction revenues (~40% of Ratings) driven by global debt issuance volumes — pent-up demand from 2022-2023 rate-hike suppression
$15-20T in global bond maturities 2025-2028 requiring refinancing
Emerging market debt growth (EM issuers increasingly requiring international credit ratings for bond market access)
Private credit rating expansion (SPGI rating private credit vehicles for institutional investors)
growth_risks: Higher-for-longer Fed Funds rate delays HY issuance recovery — transaction revenue stays below peak
European ESMA regulatory push to increase credit rating competition (limited practical impact historically)
AI-based credit scoring for small/mid-cap issuers could reduce demand for formal NRSRO ratings in some segments
Macro recession suppresses new issuance; credit quality concerns keep HY dormant
segments.crux_exposure: MEDIUM — moat is regulatory (NRSRO), not intellectual; cycle risk is real but temporary

segments.name: S&P Global Commodity Insights (Platts)
growth_drivers: Platts crude oil, natural gas, LNG, and metals benchmark prices are physically contractually embedded (spot markets, futures settlement, physical contracts)
Energy transition creates new benchmarks: carbon credits (ETS, voluntary), critical minerals (lithium, cobalt, nickel), green hydrogen
LNG market growth: global LNG trade doubled 2010-2025, creating demand for real-time price benchmarks
Analytics products layered on top of benchmarks: margin analytics, supply chain visibility, scenario analysis
growth_risks: ICE (Intercontinental Exchange) and CME Group are building competing commodity data and analytics capabilities
If fossil fuel demand declines faster than expected, legacy oil/gas benchmark volumes could shrink
Carbon market benchmark competition from ICE, MSCI, and regional operators
segments.crux_exposure: LOW — benchmark prices are contractually embedded; moat is structural

segments.name: S&P Global Mobility
growth_drivers: VIN data (Vehicle Identification Number) and vehicle history data is embedded in dealer and insurer workflows; sticky renewal rates
EV transition complexity: OEMs and dealers need more sophisticated analytics for new powertrain configurations
Supply chain visibility tools for automotive OEMs — global chip shortage aftermath created demand for supply chain risk monitoring
Insurance pricing and risk analytics (AutoSource, insurance underwriting tools)
growth_risks: Automotive market cyclicality: new vehicle sales volumes affect both dealer software demand and OEM analytics spend
Connected vehicle data: OEM data partnerships may bypass IHS Markit's data intermediary role
China automotive market exposure where SPGI Mobility faces domestic competitors (ChinaAuto, 36kr data)
segments.crux_exposure: LOW — steady subscription; automotive data is deeply embedded in counterparty workflows

segments.name: S&P Dow Jones Indices
growth_drivers: S&P 500, S&P MidCap 400, S&P SmallCap 600 have $8.5T+ in passive AUM — revenue grows automatically with market appreciation
Net passive fund flows: $500B+ annually into US equity index funds and ETFs (ICI data)
New index products: ESG indices, factor indices, private market indices expanding licensable IP
International expansion: increasing AUM in APAC and EM indexed to SPGI benchmarks
Options market growth: S&P 500 options notional is $10T+; CME and CBOE pay royalties to SPGI for options on S&P indices
growth_risks: Equity market correction reduces AUM mechanically (2022: equity indices fell 20%, Indices revenue fell proportionally)
Passive-to-active rotation if active managers demonstrate sustained alpha (structural but slow-moving risk)
MSCI and FTSE Russell compete for new international index mandates
Regulatory pressure on index provider market concentration
segments.evidence_quality: HIGH — AUM-linked revenue is transparent and predictable
segments.crux_exposure: LOW — the S&P 500 cannot be disrupted; it IS the benchmark

segment_growth_summary.crux_segment: Market Intelligence — smallest margin differential, largest AI risk, but also largest data asset value if AI demand for proprietary data proves out

key_growth_assumptions.what_decides_it: MI subscription retention rates (next 2-3 renewals cycles) and Ratings transaction revenue recovery (rate cycle timing)
key_growth_assumptions.bias_audit: 8% CAGR base is consistent with FY2023-FY2025 trend (12.5% avg YoY, slowing post-IHS-Markit integration lift; normalized run-rate is 7-9% organic). Not stacked conservative or bullish.
